result:
Build is green. Done.

**Root cause**: `lua_vm::api::push_thread` (crates/lua-vm/src/api.rs:1233) was stubbed — it computed `is_main` and returned, but never pushed a `LuaValue::Thread` onto the stack. `debug.sethook(hook, "r")` (called from a `<close>` metamethod on line 797 of locals.lua) needs to set `hooktable[L1] = hook`, so `push_thread` followed by `push_value(hookfn)` followed by `lua_rawset(-3)` expects three pushed values. With the stub, only two values were on top of the subtable and `lua_rawset(-3)` reached down into earlier argument slots — picking up the string `"r"` and triggering `debug_assert!(matches!(t, LuaValue::Table(_)))` inside `get_table_value` at api.rs:1328.

**Fix**: Added a stable per-state `thread_token: GcRef<LuaThread>` field on `GlobalState` (initialized once via `LuaThread::placeholder()` in `new_state`), then made `push_thread` clone it and push `LuaValue::Thread(token)`. This gives a consistent hashable thread identity for registry subtables (debug hook table, etc.) while waiting on Phase-D's self-referential `GcRef<LuaState>` work. Also traced the new field in `GlobalState::trace`.

**New state**: Did not re-run the program (per instructions). `cargo build -q -p lua-cli` is clean (no errors). The mega-loop will re-test.
